rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 26, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE NOTICE OF HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is continued to January 30, 2025. Prior to the original hearing the Court issued a tentative ruling seeking clarification on two items: (1) number of exemption claims on calendar; and (2) purported November 25, 2024 filings. The Court received no satisfactory explanations for either. According to the respondent, there should have been only one hearing regarding "retirement account with Voya." However, attached to the 2 almost identical briefs filed by the respondent are 2 Claims of Exemption as to "Any and all accounts in the name of Gregory Malin held at Voaya Investment Management Co, LLC" received by the Sherriff on October 11, 2024 two minutes apart (10:27 and 10:29 listed on the Received Stamp). Based on these filings it is still unclear if there is one or two claims. As to the November 25, 2024 purported filings by the respondent, respondents asserts that the filings have been submitted to the Court on November 25, 2024 and have been rejected by the clerk on December 10, 2024 due to the filings not being made by counsel of record. Court records indicate that on November 25, 2024 only a 3-page Proof of Service was submitted for filing and no other documents. To avoid any further confusion, missing filings and potentially duplicate filings: (1) no later than January 2, 2025 petitioner to set hearings on the respondent's claim(s) of exemption attaching a copy of the claim(s) accompanied by points and authorities and evidence, if any. Petitioner shall title the document(s) "Amended Notice of Hearing on Claim of Exemption;" (2) no later than January 16, 2025 respondent to file an opposition supported accompanied by points and authorities and evidence, if any; and (3) no later than January 23, 2025 petitioner to file a reply. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: POLK STREET ASSOCIATES VS. BERBER GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 26, 2024 line 3. PLAINTIFF POLK STREET ASSOCIATES MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED, in part. Plaintiff has not met its burden of showing that every cause of action should be stricken. Paragraphs 5, 7, and 8 are stricken, to the extent that they support the claim that Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant plotted to shut down and take over from Defendant/Cross-Complainant's business. To the extent Paragraphs 5, 7, and 8 support this claim, this claim arises from the filing of a UD, which is protected activity under CCP Section 425.16. Additionally, this claim is not merely incidental to the Cross-Complaint's requested relief for Causes of Action 1, 2, 3, 4, and 6. Paragraph 9 is stricken, to the extent that it supports the claim that Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant plotted to shut down and take over Defendant/Cross-Complainant's business. To the extent that Paragraph 9 supports this claim, this claim arises from protected activity under CCP Section 425.16(e)(2) and is not merely incidental to the Cross-Complaint's requested relief for Causes of Action 1, 2, 3, 4, and 6. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1651 MARKET, LP VS. VALENTIN ISAEV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To The Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 26, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF 1651 MARKET, LP MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO THE STIPULATION is DENIED. Motion is not supported by points and authorities. The unnumbered pages attached to the Notice of Motion do not include any citation to authority and no other papers are filed with the motion include authorities. CRC 3.1112(a)(3). Motion is not supported by any admissible competent evidence regarding breach(es). Considering evidence provided in opposition, the Court finds that even if any breach was sufficiently proven by the moving party, such breach is not material. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. MOTOYOSHI KOMORI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 26, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT MOTOYOSHI KOMORI DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to January 2, 2025. No later than December 27, 2024 Plaintiff to file a declaration executed by a person who added language (See POS attached to the Second Amended Complaint) to the Proof of Service dated January 5, 2024 and singed by "Aaron Wilson." The declaration shall include the date of the change was made. Also, Plaintiff shall file a supplemental brief (not to exceed) two pages providing authorities allowing Plaintiff to (1) change the caption of the case without leave of Court; (2) make amendments in the Second Amended Complaint without leave of Court that exceed the leave to amend granted in conjunction with the prior order (July 29, 2024). No further argument shall be included. Supplemental brief shall only include authorities, of in Plaintiff does not find such authorities, the brief shall state "none." =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
